capture.cameraPose: Desktop browser full-page capture with WebXR unavailable and right-side runtime posture and Quest evidence panels visible

notes: The ED chest-pain administrative/runtime surface is visible and medically relevant text is readable, but the 3D bay itself is sparse and cannot yet convey a high-pressure emergency encounter.

The visible actor forms are primitive capsule/head proxies with no clothing, facial expression, pain response, skin detail, rigging, or realistic clinical equipment, so this does not advance production realism.
The right-side desktop panels, trace buttons, runtime posture, Quest evidence rows, and copied JSON area are readable in the full-page browser artifact.

Trace buttons and Copy Evidence are visible, but the Enter Full VR button is disabled because desktop WebXR is unavailable; this artifact does not prove controller, hand, locomotion, or headset interaction.
The left 3D scene shows cropped primitive actors and partly clipped in-canvas evidence text, which is useful as a layout warning but not a headset scale or occlusion pass.

This is a desktop Chrome DevTools screenshot for adversarial visual QA only; it is not physical Quest evidence, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, and not production runtime readiness.
